name: Lepton AI
what_it_does: AI cloud platform for running LLMs and ML models with a Python-native deployment experience. Lepton provides hosted LLM APIs (Llama 3, Mistral, Qwen, etc.) at competitive pricing, plus a deployment platform for custom Python AI applications. Notable for the LeptonAI Python SDK that treats Python functions as deployable services. Founded by ex-Meta/CMU researchers.
use_cases: Run open-source LLM inference (Llama 3, Mistral, Qwen) via OpenAI-compatible API endpoints at competitive pricing for agent inference
Deploy custom Python AI applications and agent services as Lepton 'photons' without container management
Build cost-efficient agent batch processing pipelines using Lepton's GPU infrastructure at competitive per-token pricing
Access fine-tuned or specialized open-source models via Lepton's model hub without self-hosting GPU infrastructure
Run multi-modal agent tasks (text + image) using Lepton's hosted vision models
not_for: Teams needing frontier closed models (GPT-4o, Claude 3.7) — Lepton serves open-source models only
Enterprise requiring SOC2/HIPAA compliance with BAA — Lepton's compliance posture is less established than Azure/AWS
Teams already invested in AWS/GCP ML infrastructure — Lepton requires adopting their Python SDK
best_when: You want competitive pricing for open-source LLM inference with a Python-native deployment experience and OpenAI API compatibility.
avoid_when: You need proprietary frontier models, enterprise compliance guarantees, or multi-cloud deployment flexibility.

auth.notes: API key in Authorization Bearer header. OpenAI-compatible endpoint uses same key format. Keys from Lepton dashboard.

pricing.paid_tiers: LLM API: ~$0.07-0.80/M tokens depending on model; Compute: per-second GPU billing
pricing.notes: Per-token pricing for LLM APIs competitive with Together AI and Fireworks. Compute platform charges per-second for deployed services. $10 signup credit for evaluation.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits not prominently documented; standard inference API rate limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAI-compatible error format for LLM API. Deployment platform errors surface in Lepton dashboard. Error messages are functional but documentation is less extensive than established providers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: LLM inference is idempotent at temperature=0. Agents can safely retry failed inference calls. Deployed photon endpoints are stateless by default.
known_agent_gotchas: OpenAI compatibility is good but not perfect — some advanced parameters (function calling, JSON mode) may behave differently per model
Model availability changes — Lepton adds and removes models; agents should handle model-not-found errors gracefully
Smaller team than Together AI or Fireworks — documentation and support response may be slower
Python SDK ('photon') deployment model is opinionated — requires adopting Lepton's decorator pattern for service deployment
Rate limit documentation is sparse — implement conservative rate limiting by default

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key with no scope granularity. Newer company with less established compliance certifications than major cloud providers.
